item.title: UK and France Break May Records as Heat Scorches Europe
item.description: Driven by a heat dome, temperatures have soared.

item.title: Toxic Fumes From Tehran Oil Fires Affected Area Size of Italy
item.description: When Tehran oil infrastructure caught fire in March following Israeli strikes, the blaze produced toxic fumes detectable across an area the size of Italy, according to fresh research.

item.title: NextEra’s Dominion Deal Expands Its Storm Risk
item.description: The utility giant is expanding into some of the US’ costliest weather zones.

item.title: Kenya in Talks With Karpowership for Emergency Power Supply
item.description: Kenya plans to contract Karpowership, the world’s biggest provider of ship-mounted electricity plants, to provide emergency power for the nation’s growing demands.

item.title: Europe Heat Wave Boosts Solar, Sends Power Prices Negative
item.description: The first major heat wave of the season broke temperature records across northwest Europe, triggered water shortages in the UK and has been linked to several deaths in France.

item.title: Clean Energy Trade Ripe for ‘Smart Money’, Singapore Envoy Says
item.description: Renewables are poised to attract fresh, large-scale investment as energy security and supply chain concerns drive an increase in demand and revenue for the sector, according to Singapore’s chief climate diplomat.

item.title: Saving Supply Chains From Climate Shocks Is a Lure for Investors
item.description: Technologies that help manage risks to goods from extreme temperatures and natural disasters are drawing new attention to the adaptation sector.

item.title: India’s Scorching Nights Strain Power Grid and Threaten Health
item.description: India’s dangerously torrid summer days have long been a climate warning. Now the country is increasingly dealing with sweltering nights too, as global warming and poor urban planning put millions at risk.

item.title: BHP Cuts Green Push in Iron Ore Segment, Media Report Says
item.description: BHP Group is pulling back on key decarbonization projects in its Western Australian iron ore operations, slowing a climate strategy the miner had once positioned as central to its long-term growth plans, according to leaked internal documents cited by the Guardian and ABC’s Four Corners.

item.title: A Hunt to Find Dubious Chinese Carbon Credits Snapped Up in Europe
item.description: Overvalued offsets are nothing new. But some emissions-reduction projects don’t appear to exist at all, a Bloomberg investigation found

item.title: Oil Sands Firms Can Afford Carbon Capture, Canadian Energy Minister Says
item.description: Canadian Energy Minister Tim Hodgson said he’s “highly confident” that Alberta oil sands companies can absorb the cost of building carbon capture, despite their protests that Canada’s climate rules hurt their global competitiveness.

item.title: Dubious Chinese Carbon Credits Expose European Market’s Flaws
item.description: Companies in at least nine European countries bought carbon offsets from projects that appear not to exist or were verified by an auditor-turned-developer who treated the two jobs as a revolving door, a Bloomberg investigation found.

item.title: EPA Says California Chemical Tank Likely to Leak, Not Explode
item.description: A compromised chemical tank in California that has prompted the evacuation of more than 40,000 people is more likely to leak into its surroundings than explode, Environmental Protection Agency Administrator Lee Zeldin said.

item.title: ‘Move-in Ready’ Means Green for This City’s Homebuyers
item.description: Berkeley, California, now requires that homes for sale meet an emissions-saving threshold.

item.title: France Seeks to Shield Energy Producers From ESG Fund Regulation
item.description: France wants incoming ESG fund rules to be adjusted so that sustainable investors don’t need to exclude key energy producers, according to a draft proposal seen by Bloomberg.

item.title: Orsted Considers Selling US Renewable Assets Worth More Than $1 Billion
item.description: Orsted A/S is exploring a sale of its onshore renewables assets in the US, people with knowledge of the matter said, as part of a pivot to its core market of offshore wind in Europe.

item.title: Climeworks CEO Says Carbon Removal Can’t Hinge on Microsoft
item.description: A pullback by the tech giant could spur an evolution of the market, said Christoph Gebald.

item.title: Europe’s First Major Heat Wave of Summer Threatens Records
item.description: The first heat wave of the summer is set to scorch Europe over the coming week, sending temperatures toward monthly records in the UK, France and Spain.

item.title: Middle East War Risks Creating an Environmental Disaster, Experts Warn
item.description: There’s been an increasing amount of oil slicks.

item.title: Trump to Delay Superpollutant Restrictions in Affordability Push
item.description: The Trump administration on Thursday intends to delay compliance with two rules on refrigerants as it looks to dismantle former President Joe Biden’s environmental agenda.

item.title: UK’s Mandate for Solar in Car Parks Scrapped Over Cost Concerns
item.description: The UK government shelved plans to require new car parks to fit solar panels, after finding it may not deliver the savings first hoped for.

item.title: France Floods Europe’s Grid With Solar, Pushing Prices Negative
item.description: French solar power surged on Thursday as a stretch of sunny weather boosted generation allowing the country to export huge volumes of electricity to neighboring markets.

item.title: Shareholders Press Big Tech Over AI Energy Use and Climate Goals
item.description: Activist investors want Amazon, Google and Meta to explain how they’ll reconcile power-hungry data centers with their pledges to cut emissions.

item.title: Satellite Images Show Persian Gulf Oil Slicks Growing During War
item.description: Attacks on ships and oil infrastructure risk creating environmental disaster, experts warn.

item.title: Namibia Secures $60 Million Funding Under New Conservation Model
item.description: Namibia secured more than N$1 billion ($60 million) in financing from a new conservation initiative that aims at protecting wildlife and sustaining rural livelihoods as traditional donor funding declines.

item.title: Top Climate Target Verifier Adopts More Flexible Global Approach
item.description: The world’s largest verifier of corporate climate goals is updating its approach to give greater recognition to the challenges companies face in decarbonizing.

item.title: Coal Power Capacity Grew in 2025 Despite Dip in Use, GEM Says
item.description: Global coal-fired generation capacity grew last year, despite a drop in use of the dirty fossil fuel as nations turn increasingly to renewables to meet new demand for electricity.

item.title: Record Heat Forces New England to Tap Oil to Power Electric Grid
item.description: Record heat in May has forced New England’s electric grid to turn to oil — a dirty fuel source the region typically only uses in bouts of extreme weather.

item.title: Canada Updates Federal Carbon Price to Match Lower Alberta Level
item.description: Canada is applying a slower escalator for its national carbon price to match a deal it struck with the oil-rich province of Alberta, according to an updated government website.

item.title: Bank Activist Group Shuts Down, Cites Limits of Climate Push
item.description: A shareholder activist group in Canada is shutting down after concluding that investor pressure alone isn’t enough to meaningfully influence financial and energy firms to address climate risks.
